perturbation.original_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement related to trade secrets, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. For all other breaches of this agreement, money damages shall be the sole remedy.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting injunctive relief to breaches related to trade secrets only, while stating that money damages are the *sole* remedy for all other breaches. This directly contradicts the initial statement that injunctive relief is available for *any* breach. This creates ambiguity regarding the remedies available for breaches not involving trade secrets. This contradicts the first part of the sentence in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies. It is agreed that money damages would be an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement related to trade secrets, and the non-breaching party shall be entitled to injunctive relief, specific performance, and/or any other appropriate equitable remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. For all other breaches of this agreement, money damages shall be the sole remedy. Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 by limiting injunctive relief and specific performance to breaches related to trade secrets only, while stating that money damages are the sole remedy for all other breaches. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case, but importantly used to show where the contradiction lies) includes the statement that money damages are the *sole* remedy for other breaches and also includes the sentence 'Such remedies shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.' This directly contradicts the statement that money damages are the *sole* remedy, as it suggests other remedies might still be available, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction within Section 6 itself. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, without consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and can be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part at any time with verbal approval. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, without consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement can be modified, released, discharged, or terminated at any time with verbal approval, while the preceding clause required written approval. This creates ambiguity about the proper method for modifying the contract. This is contradictory to Section 9 in the first part of the sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and can be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part at any time with verbal approval. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, without consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9 to allow verbal approval for modifications, releases, discharges, or termination of the agreement, which directly contradicts the original requirement for written approval. The contradicted text is the same section that was modified, creating an in-text contradiction. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Term. This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the parties and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date thereof, provided however, that the obligations of the parties contained herein shall continue for a period of five (5) years following the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Term. This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the parties and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date thereof, provided however, that the obligations of the parties contained herein shall continue for a period of one (1) year following the date of such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of obligations after termination. The section initially states the obligations continue for five years, but the change reduces this period to one year. This inconsistency creates confusion and uncertainty regarding the post-termination obligations of the parties. This is contradictory to Section 11 in the last part of the sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Term. This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the parties and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date thereof, provided however, that the obligations of the parties contained herein shall continue for a period of one (1) year following the date of such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 11, reducing the period for which obligations continue after termination from five years to one year. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text but since this section originally stated five years, but now states one year, there is a contradiction within Section 11 and with the Original Text, therefore the contradiction exists.
